replies.author: @LunarEleven
replies.text:
​@neo_child So apparently the mistake was letting women choose a game for a women's event. Mmmmkay. If a panel of men chose it, I could see people thinking it's a blunder even if they chose it because of its popularity with women. It could certainly be misunderstood. But as soon as you learn that a panel of women chose it, isn't it time to stfu? 

At that point you're literally just saying women are not competent to organize a women's event. Seems pretty counterintuitive. It's not progressive to say that because you're a woman you have to base your choices on public perception and you need to be competing in male dominated games to PROVE something. 

Gender equality is not about being afraid to like things that are associated with your gender. It means you don't HAVE to, you're not limited to that. If you think you need to be competing with men for feminism, you're still centering men in your own idea of womanhood. It is performative and not in any way productive.
